title: Optimal Use of Insurance in a Transnational Mining Company: Insurability Boundaries, Contract Span, and Governance-Tail Residuals
author: Bell, Peter
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This paper introduces two auditable protocols—PCMP (Product-to-Claim) and CPMP (Claim-to-Product)—to map transnational mining tail events and firm exposures into standardized “coverage architecture” memos. It formalizes an insurability-boundary view of why some governance-tail loss vectors (especially repricing/feasibility, legality constraints, and verification-dominant disputes) remain only partially spanned by standard insurance products. Includes a six-figure instrument pack and worked CPMP example.
steps_raw: This paper introduces two auditable protocols—PCMP (Product-to-Claim) and CPMP (Claim-to-Product)—to map transnational mining tail events and firm exposures into standardized “coverage architecture” memos. Includes a six-figure instrument pack and worked CPMP example.
